Tests Within Level: Intro A, Intro B, Intro C
Focus: Establish rhythm, relaxation, and basic straightness at walk and trot.
Representative Test Movements:
Intro A: Walk work, halt, large circles. 
Intro B: Adds trot, basic transitions. 
Intro C: Sustained trot, 20m circles both directions.
Key RiderBiomechanics: Rein symmetry, Rein steadiness, Core stability, Lower leg stability

Focus: Develop steady contact and introduce balanced canter work.
Representative Test Movements:
Test 1: 20m circles (trot & canter), free walk. 
Test 2: Lengthening at trot, more transitions. 
Test 3: Clear lengthenings, improved canter balance.
Key RiderBiomechanics: Rein steadiness, Lower leg stability, Upper body alignment, Core stability

Focus: Improve bend, balance, and introduce lateral work.
Representative Test Movements:
Test 1: 15m trot circles, leg yield, simple changes. 
Test 2: More demanding leg yield, lengthenings. 
Test 3: Increased precision in lateral work.
Key RiderBiomechanics: Weight distribution, Core stability, Seat independence, Rein symmetry

Representative Test Movements:
Test 1: Shoulder-in, travers, rein back, counter canter. 
Test 2: Medium gaits, increased collection moments. 
Test 3: Greater precision in collected and lateral work.
Key RiderBiomechanics: Half-halt coordination, Timing of aids, Pelvis neutrality, Rein steadiness

Focus: Establish sustained collection and advanced lateral work.
Representative Test Movements:
Test 1: Half-pass, single flying changes. 
Test 2: More demanding half-pass and changes. 
Test 3: Increased expression and consistency.
Key RiderBiomechanics: Half-halt coordination, Core stability, Seat independence, Upper body alignment
